# Pegasus IA workspace instructions

This workspace uses the Pegasus IA VS Code/Copilot harness. The harness provides local workflow, documentation, and MCP-first operational memory. It does not authorize framework scaffolding, GitHub setup, CI, deployment, database setup, or business/domain application code by itself.

## Primary entry point

Use `.github/agents/pegasus-orchestrator.agent.md` as the primary Copilot agent for project work. The orchestrator coordinates SDD phase agents and selected specialist agents through Copilot custom-agent handoffs where supported.

Follow `.github/instructions/pegasus-sdd-boundaries.instructions.md` for the centralized artifact-language contract. Agent-consumed artifacts default to English unless the user explicitly names another language for the artifact. Chat, persona, dominant source language, and prior artifacts do not select artifact language.

The orchestrator is a thin coordinator, not a phase executor. Every SDD phase is delegated to the matching specialized agent in a fresh context; phase agents execute directly without recursive delegation. If delegation is unavailable, blocked, or fails, stop and report. The orchestrator does not write phase artifacts, implement tasks, run phase tests/builds, or verify implementation. After `sdd-design` returns, it validates only the mandatory result envelope and never rereads/revalidates the design, reruns marker/language/traceability/phase checks, or performs design persistence. Outside SDD, delegate when understanding needs 4 or more files, implementation touches 2 or more non-trivial files, tooling/tests/builds/installs must run, or complexity exceeds small mechanical coordination.

## Source of truth and memory

Follow `.github/instructions/pegasus-memory.instructions.md` as the centralized MCP memory policy.

Before changing files, call the `pegasus-memory-mcp` `health` tool before the first recovery attempt. If `health` succeeds, recover project/change context through MCP. If recovery returns `not_found` with `project_not_found`, call `ensure_project` before recording observations, artifacts, task progress, or handoffs. For active work, read `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/prd.md`, `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/proposal.md`, `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/spec.md`, `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/design.md`, `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/tasks.md`, `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/apply-progress.md`, and `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/verify.md`. Root phase files are canonical templates only, except the intentional natural root PRD entry before an active change is selected.

Natural-language PRD intent is enough to start PRD discovery. If the user describes an idea or says something like "I want to draft a PRD for this idea" / "quiero armar un PRD para esta idea", infer the PRD workflow without asking for Pegasus internals: call MCP `health` before recovery, recover/search context if healthy, ensure the project exists when recovery reports `project_not_found`, ask one concise round of key product/business questions before editing or finalizing when product decisions are open, never silently decide product scope, draft or refine `docs/pegasus/prd.md` or `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/prd.md`, validate the PRD by reading it back, before any git command first check for `.git` and never run `git diff`, `git status`, or other git validation in non-git workspaces; do not try git first and fall back, tell the user the PRD file path and ask them to review it, call `ensure_change` before recording a new change PRD under `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/prd.md` using only `project_id` and `change_id` by default; add `key`, `title`, `status`, or `description` only when needed, and use `kind` only if classification is needed. Never send `type` or both `kind` and `type`, even if equal; never send nested `metadata`, arrays, decisions, questions, or artifact summaries to `ensure_change`, save PRD status/decisions/questions and artifact reference through MCP after `health` succeeds with `record_observation` and `record_artifact`, include a small MCP persistence summary marking `ensure_project`, `ensure_change`, `record_artifact`, and `record_observation` as `succeeded`, `not needed`, or `failed: <reason>`, say the PRD is file-only if required artifact or observation persistence failed, wait for explicit PRD approval before proposal/spec/design/tasks/apply, and do not implement code during PRD flow.

Before proposal, inspect the referenced PRD file rather than relying on conversational approval. Its Approval table/status must be `Approved`; when an approval checkbox is present, it must be checked and agree with the table. If the artifact remains Draft or inconsistent, stop and ask to update/approve it. Every proposal product claim must be traceable to explicit PRD text: do not turn unstated details into preserved PRD assumptions. Ask about material omissions before finalizing, or record the exact unresolved gap and impact without inventing a default. New change-scoped proposals must use the actual `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/proposal.md` path in both Pegasus managed markers. The final proposal response must include the exact `MCP persistence summary:` block with lines for `ensure_project`, `ensure_change`, `record_artifact`, `record_observation`, `record_task_progress`, and `record_handoff`, each as `succeeded`, `not needed`, or `failed: <reason>`, even if MCP is unavailable; failed required artifact or observation persistence also requires `Proposal persistence: file-only — <reason>`.

Before spec, inspect the current change's PRD and proposal and validate in-file approval: conversational approval cannot override Draft, Pending, unchecked, or inconsistent artifacts, and any present table/status/checkbox indicators must agree. They are the only default product and requirements sources. Do not inspect or reuse neighboring changes for requirements, scenarios, wording, style, or formatting unless an explicit PRD, Pegasus Memory, or user dependency exists; disclose permitted use and that it was not an implicit scope source. Reconcile material requirements/acceptance gaps with reliable current-change evidence or a user answer, otherwise retain owner, impact, next step, and needed-by gate; ambiguous Pegasus Memory context never resolves a gap and a blocking gap stops finalization. Every normative requirement must trace to approved PRD/proposal evidence or a visible unresolved gap. A new change-scoped spec must use exact first/last markers for `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/spec.md`; reread, repair, and validate markers before Pegasus Memory persistence. When healthy, call or attempt `record_task_progress` before `record_handoff`. For a successfully drafted spec ready for user review, use status `completed` on the first attempt; record phase `spec`, artifact path, `ready for review` / draft complete, open gaps/blockers, and next action `user review/approval` in descriptive fields or notes. The supported status enum is exactly `pending`, `in_progress`, `blocked`, `completed`: use `blocked` when blocked, `in_progress` for active work, and `pending` for work not yet started. Never send `ready-for-review` or `completed-as-draft` as a status. Do not finalize until each of the six Pegasus Memory operations has a truthful terminal status; never invent `succeeded` for an omitted call. The final spec response must include the exact six-line `Pegasus Memory persistence summary:` block even when unavailable. `record_artifact` or `record_observation` failure requires `Spec persistence: file-only — <reason>`; if both succeeded but `record_task_progress` or `record_handoff` fails, require `Pegasus Memory persistence incomplete/partial — <failed operation>: <reason>`. Any failed required closure operation forbids a full durable-completion or Pegasus Memory-success claim. Spec is acceptance-only: no architecture, tasks, or implementation.

For spec work, select one artifact language before writing: an explicit user instruction naming the spec language wins; otherwise use English. Chat, persona, dominant approved-source language, and prior artifact language never override this contract. After exact marker validation and before Pegasus Memory persistence, run a separate language/terminology gate. When Spanish was explicitly selected, scan structural labels: require `Creado:` and `Destino:`; reject `Created:`, `Target:`, and every applicable default-English canonical heading/table label from the canonical spec-template vocabulary. The structural scan allows standardized `GIVEN` / `WHEN` / `THEN`, contractually required canonical enum values such as `Approved` or `Draft`, paths, identifiers, tool/server names, code, source-section references, and established technical terms. Repair only affected language blocks, reread the whole artifact, revalidate markers, and rerun the gate. `Language gate: passed` is forbidden while any prohibited English structural label remains. Spanish must translate all human-readable headings, table labels, metadata labels, and prose into neutral, professional Spanish; only immutable identifiers, deliberately standardized normative keywords, code, paths, and tool names may remain unchanged. If unresolved, report every exact issue, do not persist or claim success, and state `Spec persistence: file-only — language validation failed: <exact issues>`. State `Artifact language: <selected language>` and `Language gate: <passed|blocked: exact unresolved issues>` before the exact Pegasus Memory persistence summary.

Before design, require current-change in-file approval for PRD, proposal, and spec; do not let conversation override a missing, Draft, Pending, unchecked, or inconsistent artifact. The active design is `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/design.md`; root `docs/pegasus/design.md` is a canonical template only. Read relevant code, architecture, and configuration when repository implementation evidence exists, then record either `existing system with implementation evidence` or the artifact-language-specific Greenfield classification. Reconcile material platform/runtime/framework, existing-stack, integration, persistence, and deployment gaps before writing. An approval/source blocker or blocking gap stops artifact writing/finalization/persistence but permits minimal Pegasus Memory control-state persistence: ensure project/change, blocker observation, phase `design` progress `blocked`, then handoff, with `record_artifact: not needed` because no artifact was written. Do not invent files or stack precision without evidence. Preserve/re-read/repair/revalidate exact change-scoped design markers and run the design language gate. Reconcile every mentioned gap before persistence; never ask a required close-out question after persistence. Use Pegasus Memory, not generic MCP terminology, for design persistence. Record `record_task_progress` before `record_handoff`, using only `pending`, `in_progress`, `blocked`, `completed`; `completed` means ready for review with no blocking gaps and `blocked` means a blocker remains. Narrative prose is insufficient: the final design response includes exact structured labels `Artifact language:`, `Language gate:`, `Deferred technical choices:`, and the exact six-state `Pegasus Memory persistence summary:` even when unavailable. Design must not create tasks, work units, or implementation code. `sdd-design` is the sole design writer, validator, and persistence owner. Its mandatory result envelope reports status, artifact path, artifact language, explicit non-English override evidence, language gate, deferred choices, marker/per-entry traceability validation, initial recovery result, later recovery/ensure transitions, all six Pegasus Memory operation states, risks/blockers, next action, and observable delegation evidence. Missing fields block success. The orchestrator summarizes this envelope, does not claim direct artifact validation, and explicitly asks for design approval before tasks.

Record implementation status in `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/apply-progress.md`, record verification in `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/verify.md`, and call `health` before the first MCP save attempt. Root phase files are canonical templates only; root PRD is a natural-entry template only before an active change is selected. When MCP is healthy, proactively save durable decisions, bugfixes, discoveries/gotchas, conventions/patterns, config/environment changes, user constraints/preferences, artifact status, task progress/blockers, verification evidence, handoffs, and session summaries through MCP. Merge updates into existing useful history; do not overwrite prior progress, apply-progress, memory, or verification evidence.

Record the project-selected Copilot model preference through MCP after `health` succeeds or through workspace settings when available. Use one model for all phases in this first release; do not promise per-phase model routing or hard runtime control from Pegasus docs alone.

Use MCP tool inputs, outputs, and documented capabilities as the memory contract only. Do not rely on `pegasus-memory-mcp` implementation details. Treat failed or missing `health` as MCP unavailable and show exactly: `El pegasus-memory-mcp no se encuentra disponible, si continuamos con eso asi, no se guardara nada de lo que hagamos en memoria persistente`. Artifact work may continue, but persistent memory saves are unavailable and there is no Markdown memory fallback.

Keep consumer states distinct: `not_found` means MCP is available but has no matching context; `project_not_found` under `not_found` requires `ensure_project` before writes; `ambiguous` means MCP is available but needs disambiguation; `read_error` is a failed read; `persistence_error` and foreign-key write failures are precondition/flow bugs, usually missing `ensure_project` or `ensure_change`. Do not collapse those states into unavailability or show the unavailable warning for them.

If MCP active-context recovery is ambiguous, do not ask the user to resolve MCP recovery details. Continue from available project artifacts and record external `pegasus-memory-mcp` follow-up when possible. `docs/pegasus/memory/` is deprecated after MCP integration and must not be treated as an active backend, fallback, or co-source.

## Design deferred-choice and language gate

Before marker validation, language validation, or Pegasus Memory persistence, reconcile every material technical gap and deferred choice. A deferred choice belongs in a dedicated `Deferred Technical Choices` table and requires choice/topic, `deferred-non-blocking` status (or selected-language translation), owner, impact, next step, needed-by gate, invariant architecture, why non-blocking, and evidence/source. Missing fields block completed/ready-for-review status; when none exist, state `None` / `Ninguna`, never `TBD`. In Greenfield context without concrete implementation stack, framework, or runtime evidence, `None` / `Ninguna` is invalid: defer stack/framework/runtime selection structurally until before tasks/apply while preserving logical components, responsibilities, boundaries, interfaces, and control flow independently of that choice. Progress notes and the final response summarize deferred choices and their next gate.

For English design artifacts, use `Greenfield / no implementation evidence`; for Spanish design artifacts, reject both `Greenfield/no implementation evidence` and `Greenfield / no implementation evidence` and use `Greenfield / sin evidencia de implementación`. Spanish requires the exact heading `Decisiones y compensaciones` and rejects `Tradeoffs`, `Costos y compromisos`, `Compensaciones`, and `Decisiones y costos y compromisos` as headings. Allow only justified immutable technical exceptions: markers, identifiers, code, paths, tool/server names, and deliberately standardized terms. When the persistence product is meant, use only `Pegasus Memory` or exact server annotation `pegasus-memory-mcp`; reject standalone/generic `MCP`, `Contexto MCP`, `Memoria MCP`, and `Memoria Pegasus`. Allow `MCP` only in an explicit protocol discussion such as `protocolo MCP`, or inside the exact server annotation. Repair affected blocks, reread the artifact, revalidate markers, and rerun the language gate before persistence.

Each `MCP` occurrence is checked independently. Only exact `protocolo MCP` and exact `pegasus-memory-mcp` are allowed in a design artifact; an allowed occurrence never permits a separate standalone `MCP` elsewhere.

## Operating boundaries

- Keep work local-first and reversible.
- Do not create app code, remotes, CI, deployment, database, framework scaffolding, MCP services, or network dependencies unless the local SDD docs explicitly request them.
- Preserve user files unless overwrite behavior is explicitly approved.
- Upgrade/sync may update Pegasus IA configurations, generated prompts/agents, and Pegasus Memory binary/config references, but must not reset, delete, recreate, or overwrite the Pegasus Memory database. Only Pegasus Memory itself may mutate the database for an explicit schema migration when it detects or ships a newer schema version; clean test memory must be explicit test setup, not a sync side effect.
- Use the direct-fix path for small, punctual, low-risk changes; use SDD for broader, ambiguous, architectural, or higher-risk changes.
- Before delegating or starting a phase/task, check MCP task progress and `docs/pegasus/changes/<change-id>/apply-progress.md` for the same phase/task already in progress or completed, and avoid duplicate launches.
- Session preflight sets review budget and delivery preference only. `sdd-tasks` later emits the Review Workload Forecast. After tasks and before apply, stop for a user decision when the forecast exceeds budget, is High risk, recommends chaining, or says a decision is needed; offer `stacked-to-main`, `feature-branch-chain`, or maintainer-approved `size:exception` without choosing silently. Apply requires the resolved strategy and one authorized slice; verify runs separately in fresh context.
- For verification, use fresh context when possible by re-reading PRD, proposal, spec, design, tasks, apply-progress, verify log, and changed files before judging completion. Before any git command, first check for `.git`; in non-git workspaces, never run `git diff`, `git status`, or other git validation and validate artifacts by reading them directly.

Cursor files under `.cursor/rules/` are legacy compatibility guidance. Prefer the Copilot assets in `.github/` for VS Code/Copilot sessions.
